title: Limit switch statement case count
description: Switch statements with too many case clauses often indicate an attempt to map data that would be better handled with a map structure. Large switch statements are harder to read and maintain than proper data structures.
recommendation: Replace large switch statements with map structures for better readability and maintainability.

noncompliant.code:
// Noncompliant: Switch statement with too many cases
func getStatusMessage(statusCode int) string {
    switch statusCode {
    case 100:
        return "Continue"
    case 101:
        return "Switching Protocols"
    case 200:
        return "OK"
    case 201:
        return "Created"
    case 202:
        return "Accepted"
    case 204:
        return "No Content"
    case 301:
        return "Moved Permanently"
    case 302:
        return "Found"
    case 304:
        return "Not Modified"
    case 400:
        return "Bad Request"
    case 401:
        return "Unauthorized"
    case 403:
        return "Forbidden"
    case 404:
        return "Not Found"
    case 405:
        return "Method Not Allowed"
    case 409:
        return "Conflict"
    case 410:
        return "Gone"
    case 422:
        return "Unprocessable Entity"
    case 429:
        return "Too Many Requests"
    case 500:
        return "Internal Server Error"
    case 501:
        return "Not Implemented"
    case 502:
        return "Bad Gateway"
    case 503:
        return "Service Unavailable"
    case 504:
        return "Gateway Timeout"
    default:
        return "Unknown Status"
    }
}

// Noncompliant: Large switch for user permissions
func hasPermission(role string, action string) bool {
    switch role + "_" + action {
    case "admin_create":
        return true
    case "admin_read":
        return true
    case "admin_update":
        return true
    case "admin_delete":
        return true
    case "editor_create":
        return true
    case "editor_read":
        return true
    case "editor_update":
        return true
    case "editor_delete":
        return false
    case "viewer_create":
        return false
    case "viewer_read":
        return true
    case "viewer_update":
        return false
    case "viewer_delete":
        return false
    case "guest_create":
        return false
    case "guest_read":
        return false
    case "guest_update":
        return false
    case "guest_delete":
        return false
    default:
        return false
    }
}
noncompliant.description: Large switch statements with many case clauses are hard to read and maintain, and often indicate that a map-based approach would be more appropriate.

compliant.description: Using maps, structs, and interfaces instead of large switch statements provides better maintainability, readability, and extensibility.

checks.message: Switch statements should not have more than 30 case clauses for maintainability.
